name: Salt Cod Fritters
intro: Preserving fish is very common around the world. We usually use mahi doodi, a dried smoked fish that can be found hanging in every grocery shop in Iran, especially around the time of Persian New Year, as it is the main focus of the annual feast. Personally, I’m not a big fan of it, but I do like using salt cod at home for a variety of dishes. My favourite is these delicate little fritters that are packed full of flavour and are very similar to those of Spanish and Portuguese origin. They make a great salty snack with drinks or as mezze with friends. I can’t stop eating them until they are all gone.

ingredients.item: salt cod (or salted pollock, which is sometimes sold in supermarkets)
packet of flat leaf parsley, leaves picked and finely chopped
packet of dill, leaves and stalks finely chopped
packet of chives, snipped or thinly sliced
large red chillies, finely chopped (with or without seeds)
bunch of spring onions, thinly sliced
finely grated rind of 2 unwaxed limes

method: Soak the salt cod in plenty of cold water. I usually soak mine for a minimum of 4–6 hours, refreshing the water every couple of hours to get rid of the excess salt. Once done, drain and dry the fish fillets completely using some kitchen paper to absorb excess moisture.

Put the mashed potato and salt cod into a large mixing bowl and break down the cod using a fork. Add the herbs, chillies, spring onions, garlic, lime rind and dry spices, followed by the egg, sea salt and black pepper to taste, and, using a wooden spoon, mix well until everything is evenly combined. Add the flour and baking powder and stir again. Cover the bowl with clingfilm and refrigerate the mixture for at least 1 hour to get it nice and cold.

Put the oil in a large saucepan over a medium-high heat and allow it to get nice and hot, but do not let it smoke. Test a tiny bit of the mixture in the oil – if the oil sizzles within a couple of seconds, it is ready to use. Take a spoonful of the mixture at a time and drop it into the oil. Don’t worry about the fritters being of uneven shapes, as this is perfectly fine. Fry the fritters for about 2–3 minutes, then turn them over and fry them for about 1 minute on the reverse side. Remove them using a slotted spoon and drain on a plate lined with kitchen paper. Repeat until all the fritters are cooked. Serve with lime wedges.
